title: lepechinia salviae
intro: Lepechinia salviae, also known as Mountain Hyssop, is a flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lepechinia salviae include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and calming digestive issues. Lepechinia salviae can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
